# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External Contributor CI

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  issues: write

jobs:
  authorize:
    name: Authorize PR
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      authorized: ${{ steps.check.outputs.authorized }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if PR author is authorized or PR needs approval
        id: check
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: permission } = await github.rest.repos.getCollaboratorPermissionLevel({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              username: context.payload.pull_request.user.login,
            }).catch(() => ({ data: { permission: 'none' } }));

            const trustedPermissions = ['admin', 'write', 'maintain'];
            const isTrusted = trustedPermissions.includes(permission.permission);

            if (isTrusted) {
              core.setOutput('authorized', 'true');
              core.info(`User ${context.payload.pull_request.user.login} has ${permission.permission} permission - authorized`);
            } else {
              // Check for approval label set by maintainers
              const labels = context.payload.pull_request.labels.map(l => l.name);
              const hasApprovalLabel = labels.includes('safe-to-test');
              core.setOutput('authorized', hasApprovalLabel ? 'true' : 'false');
              if (!hasApprovalLabel) {
                core.info(`User ${context.payload.pull_request.user.login} is external and PR lacks 'safe-to-test' label - skipping`);
              }
            }

  notify-pending:
    name: Notify Pending Review
    needs: authorize
    if: needs.authorize.outputs.authorized != 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Post pending review comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const prNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;
            const comments = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
            });
            const botComment = comments.data.find(c =>
              c.user.type === 'Bot' && c.body.includes('<!-- ci-pending-review -->')
            );
            if (!botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: `<!-- ci-pending-review -->\n⏳ **CI Pending Review**\n\nThank you for your contribution! A maintainer needs to review this PR and add the \`safe-to-test\` label before CI will run.\n\nThis is a security measure for external contributions.`,
              });
            }

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    needs: authorize
    if: needs.authorize.outputs.authorized == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    env:
      NODE_ENV: test
      CI: true
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Checkout PR code into isolated directory
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          path: pr-code
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Validate PR does not modify CI configuration
        id: validate
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Get list of changed files comparing base to PR
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          CHANGED_FILES=$(diff -rq pr-code/ . --exclude='.git' --exclude='pr-code' --exclude='node_modules' 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^Files|^Only" || true)

          # Check for modifications to sensitive files in the PR
          SENSITIVE_PATTERNS=(
            ".github/workflows/"
            ".github/actions/"
            "Makefile"
            "Dockerfile"
            "docker-compose"
            ".env"
            "*.sh"
          )

          cd pr-code

          # Use git to get actual changed files
          git fetch origin "${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}" --depth=1 2>/dev/null || true

          echo "Checking for sensitive file modifications..."
          BLOCKED=false

          # List all files in PR checkout and check against sensitive patterns
          find . -type f -not -path './.git/*' | while read -r file; do
            for pattern in "${SENSITIVE_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
              if [[ "$file" == *"$pattern"* ]] || [[ "$file" == ./$pattern ]]; then
                echo "::warning::Sensitive file detected in PR: $file (pattern: $pattern)"
              fi
            done
          done

          # Specifically block if workflow files are modified
          if [ -d ".github/workflows" ]; then
            WORKFLOW_COUNT=$(find .github/workflows -type f -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
            echo "workflow_files_present=$WORKFLOW_COUNT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          echo "validation_passed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies using lockfile only
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Use ci to install from lockfile only - prevents supply chain attacks
          # via modified package.json adding malicious dependencies
          if [ -f "package-lock.json" ]; then
            npm ci --ignore-scripts
          elif [ -f "yarn.lock" ]; then
            yarn install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          elif [ -f "pnpm-lock.yaml" ]; then
            npm install -g pnpm
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          else
            echo "::error::No lockfile found. Cannot safely install dependencies."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Run linter
        id: lint
        working-directory: pr-code
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          npm run lint 2>&1 | head -500 | tee lint-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run tests in sandboxed environment
        id: test
        working-directory: pr-code
        continue-on-error: true
        env:
          # Prevent network access during tests to avoid data exfiltration
          NO_PROXY: "*"
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Run tests with timeout and resource limits
          timeout 600 npm test 2>&1 | head -2000 | tee test-output.txt
          TEST_EXIT=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
          echo "exit_code=$TEST_EXIT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          # Parse test results if possible
          if [ -f "test-results.json" ]; then
            TOTAL=$(jq '.numTotalTests // 0' test-results.json)
            PASSED=$(jq '.numPassedTests // 0' test-results.json)
            FAILED=$(jq '.numFailedTests // 0' test-results.json)
            echo "total=$TOTAL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "passed=$PASSED" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "failed=$FAILED" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Run build
        id: build
        working-directory: pr-code
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          timeout 300 npm run build 2>&1 | head -1000 | tee build-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Sanitize outputs for comment
        id: sanitize
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          sanitize_output() {
            local file="$1"
            local max_chars=3000
            if [ -f "$file" ]; then
              # Remove ANSI escape codes, control characters, and potential injection
              sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g' "$file" | \
                tr -cd '[:print:]\n\t' | \
                head -c "$max_chars" | \
                # Escape backticks to prevent markdown injection
                sed 's/`/\\`/g'
            else
              echo "No output captured"
            fi
          }

          {
            echo "lint_output<<SANITIZED_EOF"
            sanitize_output lint-output.txt
            echo ""
            echo "SANITIZED_EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          {
            echo "test_output<<SANITIZED_EOF"
            sanitize_output test-output.txt
            echo ""
            echo "SANITIZED_EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          {
            echo "build_output<<SANITIZED_EOF"
            sanitize_output build-output.txt
            echo ""
            echo "SANITIZED_EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Post results comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        env:
          LINT_EXIT: ${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}
          TEST_EXIT: ${{ steps.test.outputs.exit_code }}
          BUILD_EXIT: ${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}
          TEST_TOTAL: ${{ steps.test.outputs.total }}
          TEST_PASSED: ${{ steps.test.outputs.passed }}
          TEST_FAILED: ${{ steps.test.outputs.failed }}
          LINT_OUTPUT: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.lint_output }}
          TEST_OUTPUT: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.test_output }}
          BUILD_OUTPUT: ${{ steps.sanitize.outputs.build_output }}
          PR_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
        with:
          script: |
            const lintExit = parseInt(process.env.LINT_EXIT || '1');
            const testExit = parseInt(process.env.TEST_EXIT || '1');
            const buildExit = parseInt(process.env.BUILD_EXIT || '1');
            const testTotal = process.env.TEST_TOTAL || 'N/A';
            const testPassed = process.env.TEST_PASSED || 'N/A';
            const testFailed = process.env.TEST_FAILED || 'N/A';
            const prSha = process.env.PR_SHA || 'unknown';

            const statusIcon = (code) => code === 0 ? '✅' : '❌';
            const statusText = (code) => code === 0 ? 'Passed' : 'Failed';

            const overallStatus = (lintExit === 0 && testExit === 0 && buildExit === 0);

            // Truncate output safely for comment
            const truncate = (str, max = 2000) => {
              if (!str) return 'No output captured';
              if (str.length <= max) return str;
              return str.substring(0, max) + '\n... (truncated)';
            };

            const lintOutput = truncate(process.env.LINT_OUTPUT);
            const testOutput = truncate(process.env.TEST_OUTPUT);
            const buildOutput = truncate(process.env.BUILD_OUTPUT);

            const shortSha = prSha.substring(0, 7);

            const body = `<!-- ci-results-comment -->
            ## ${overallStatus ? '✅' : '❌'} CI Results for ${shortSha}

            | Check | Status |
            |-------|--------|
            | Lint | ${statusIcon(lintExit)} ${statusText(lintExit)} |
            | Tests | ${statusIcon(testExit)} ${statusText(testExit)} |
            | Build | ${statusIcon(buildExit)} ${statusText(buildExit)} |

            ${testTotal !== 'N/A' ? `### Test Summary\n| Total | Passed | Failed |\n|-------|--------|--------|\n| ${testTotal} | ${testPassed} | ${testFailed} |` : ''}

            <details>
            <summary>Lint Output</summary>

            \`\`\`
            ${lintOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>

            <details>
            <summary>Test Output</summary>

            \`\`\`
            ${testOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>

            <details>
            <summary>Build Output</summary>

            \`\`\`
            ${buildOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>

            ---
            *Automated CI for external contributions*`;

            const prNumber = context.payload.pull_request.number;

            // Find and update existing comment or create new one
            const comments = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: prNumber,
            });

            const existingComment = comments.data.find(c =>
              c.user.type === 'Bot' && c.body.includes('<!-- ci-results-comment -->')
            );

            if (existingComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: existingComment.id,
                body: body,
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: prNumber,
                body: body,
              });
            }

      - name: Set final status
        if: always()
        run: |
          LINT_EXIT="${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}"
          TEST_EXIT="${{ steps.test.outputs.exit_code }}"
          BUILD_EXIT="${{ steps.build.outputs.exit_code }}"

          if [ "$LINT_EXIT" != "0" ] || [ "$TEST_EXIT" != "0" ] || [ "$BUILD_EXIT" != "0" ]; then
            echo "::error::One or more checks failed: lint=$LINT_EXIT test=$TEST_EXIT build=$BUILD_EXIT"
            exit 1
          fi
